events.name: MET SLG AI Hackathon — Mar 2026
events.agendaSummary: Day 1: framing, agency problem pitches, team formation, build. Day 2: build, coaching, demos and judging.
events.demoDetails: Day 2, 3:00pm. Five-minute demos judged by a panel of CSA leads and agency sponsors.
events.outcomes: 7 use cases captured; 4 advanced to follow-up; 1 production candidate scoped with DOB IT.
events.lessonsLearned: Demo WiFi saturated during judging; pre-stage agency data access earlier; allocate explicit capture time on day 2.

retrospective.whatWorkedWell: Strong agency sponsor turnout and crisp problem pitches set a fast pace. Four teams had working demos by midday on day 2.
retrospective.trackFeedback: Agent track landed well — the permit assistant resonated with DOB. App Mod track felt thin; only one team chose it and needed more starter guidance.
retrospective.contentFlow: Kickoff and framing were tight. Hacking time was sufficient, but demos ran long and judging compressed the closeout.
retrospective.technicalSetup: Environment provisioning slipped — agency data access was not ready at day-1 start, costing teams early momentum. Sign-in instructions were clear once access landed.
retrospective.coachingModel: Two coaches per track worked well. Roving Data/AI coach was stretched across five teams; add one more for next MET event.
retrospective.demosJudging: Five-minute demos kept energy high. Scoring rubric was understood, but WiFi saturation during simultaneous streaming hurt a few demos.
retrospective.logisticsOps: MTC room setup was excellent. Transition between hacking and demos was rushed; pre-stage the demo network segment next time.
retrospective.teamCoordination: Microsoft CSA + partner (Contoso) handoffs were smooth. A shared roster reduced duplicate coaching.
retrospective.customerRelevance: Use cases aligned closely to DOB permitting and 311 service priorities. MTA transit use case needs a clearer owner to stay relevant.
retrospective.nextSteps: Lock the permit assistant follow-up with DOB IT; pre-provision data access at T-7; add an App Mod starter kit; recruit one more Data/AI coach.

events.name: NY State Agencies AI Hackathon — Jan 2026
events.agendaSummary: Two-day hybrid event with in-person Albany hub and remote agency participants joining via Teams.
events.demoDetails: Day 2, 2:00pm. Hybrid demos streamed to remote judges.
events.outcomes: 5 use cases captured; 2 production candidates; document-extraction pattern reused from a prior event.
events.lessonsLearned: Hybrid demo audio was inconsistent; agency data access not provisioned before day 1 (recurring blocker).

retrospective.whatWorkedWell: Reusing the document-extraction pattern as a starter kit accelerated two teams. The claims extraction demo was production-quality by day 2.
retrospective.trackFeedback: App Mod track was strong for DOL claims modernization. Agent track teams wanted more pre-built tool examples.
retrospective.contentFlow: Hybrid format kept remote agencies engaged in the morning, but afternoon energy dipped for remote participants during hacking.
retrospective.technicalSetup: Agency data access again not provisioned before day 1 — a recurring blocker. Remote login instructions worked, but VPN guidance was unclear for two agencies.
retrospective.coachingModel: Remote coaching over Teams was effective for App Mod but harder for whiteboard-style Agent design sessions.
retrospective.demosJudging: Hybrid demo audio was inconsistent for remote judges, affecting scoring fairness. Rubric and timing were otherwise solid.
retrospective.logisticsOps: Albany hub ran smoothly. AV ownership for the hybrid stream was unclear and needs a named owner.
retrospective.teamCoordination: Microsoft + Fabrikam alignment was good; a single shared channel reduced confusion across in-person and remote support.
retrospective.customerRelevance: Use cases mapped well to DMV, DOH, and DOL service-operations priorities; claims extraction directly addressed a DOL backlog.
retrospective.nextSteps: Provision data access at T-7; assign a dedicated AV/stream owner; require a remote demo dry-run; publish the doc-extraction starter kit library-wide.
